+--------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Frequency of Use:        | Once per application period, after all evaluations are finalized.                                                                                                                                                        |
+--------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Normal Course of Events: | 1.  The ÖİDB Officer logs into the system.                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 2.  The system dashboard shows a notification that "Final results from [Faculty Name] are approved and ready for publication."                                                                                           |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 3.  The Officer navigates to the "Publish Results" (Sonuçları Yayınla) screen.                                                                                                                                           |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 4.  The system displays a summary of the results (e.g., "Mühendislik Fakültesi - Bilgisayar Müh.: 5 Asil, 10 Yedek, 25 Red").                                                                                            |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 5.  The ÖİDB Officer clicks "Review Final Lists" to double-check the Asil/Yedek/Red lists and the Faculty Board's approval stamp (from UC-6.2).                                                                          |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 6.  After final review, the ÖİDB Officer clicks the "PUBLISH RESULTS TO STUDENTS" (Sonuçları Öğrencilere Yayınla) button.                                                                                                |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 7.  The system displays a final, critical confirmation prompt: "Bu işlem geri alınamaz. Sonuçlar tüm öğrencilere görünür kılınacak ve bildirim gönderilecektir. Onaylıyor musunuz?"                                      |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 8.  The ÖİDB Officer confirms the action.                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 9.  The system (in the background):                                                                                                                                                                                      |
|                          |     a. Updates the status of all relevant applications to "Results Published" (Postcondition 2).                                                                                                                         |
|                          |     b. Makes the final result and intibak tables visible to students (Postcondition 1).                                                                                                                                  |
|                          |     c. Triggers the automated notification queue to send emails/alerts to all applicants (Postcondition 3).                                                                                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 10. The system displays a success message: "Sonuçlar başarıyla yayınlandı ve öğrencilere bildirimler gönderildi."                                                                                                        |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 11. The use case concludes.                                                                                                                                                                                              |
+--------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Alternative Courses:     |  UC-2.5.AC.1.STEP.5 (Officer Finds Discrepancy Before Publishing):                                                                                                                                                      |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 1.  At step 5, while reviewing the final lists, the ÖİDB Officer notices a major error (e.g., quota mismatch, missing approval stamp).                                                                                   |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 2.  The Officer does not click "PUBLISH".                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 3.  The Officer clicks "Send Back to Faculty Board" (Fakülte Kuruluna Geri Gönder) and provides a reason.                                                                                                                |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 4.  The system routes the package back to the Faculty Board or Dean's Office for correction.                                                                                                                             |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 5.  The use case terminates without publishing.                                                                                                                                                                          |
+--------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Exceptions:              | -   UC-2.5.EX.1.STEP.9 (Notification Service Fails):                                                                                                                                                                     |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |     1.  At step 9.c, the system successfully publishes the results (status is updated, students can see results) but the automated email notification service fails.                                                     |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |     2.  The system logs the notification failure, but the use case concludes successfully. The primary goal (publishing) is complete, and students can still see their results via UC-1.6.                               |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |  EX.Global.1                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
+--------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Includes:                | None. (This use case triggers other use cases but doesn't include one.)                                                                                                                                                  |
+--------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Special Requirements:    | 1.  SR-1: The "PUBLISH RESULTS" action (Step 6) must be protected by an extra confirmation step (Step 7) due to its irreversible nature.                                                                                 |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 2.  SR-2: The system must log which ÖİDB Officer published the results and at what time.                                                                                                                                 |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 3.  SR-3: The system must send notifications to all applicants (Asil, Yedek, and Red).                                                                                                                                   |
+--------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Assumptions:             | The "Final Lists" presented to ÖİDB (Step 5) are the definitive lists approved by the Faculty Board (UC-6.2) and confirmed for publication (UC-6.5). ÖİDB's role here is not to change the results, but to publish them. |
+--------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Notes and Issues:        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
+--------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

1.  User interface

2.  Performance/software quality requirements

Use Case: Publish Results (UC-2.5)

-   Only authorized ÖİDB Officers may publish results.

-   The system must update each application’s status to “Results Published” correctly and consistently.

-   Publication must generate the correct result data (Asil / Yedek / Red) for each student based on the finalized evaluation list.

-   All published results must become immediately visible to students on their dashboards.

-   Batch publishing must apply the correct final status to all selected applications without partial or inconsistent updates.

-   The system must prevent republishing or altering results once they are marked as officially published.

-   All publication actions must be logged with Officer ID, timestamp, and the published application set.

    1.  Use case 6 for user 2

        1.  Use case template (use-case-template.docx file)

  ---------------- ------------------------------- -------------------- --
  Use Case ID:     UC-2.6                                               
  Use Case Name:   Review and Respond to Appeals                        
  Created By:      Group 1                         Last Updated By:     
  Date Created:    15.11.2025                      Date Last Updated:   
  ---------------- ------------------------------- -------------------- --

+--------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Actor:                   | ÖİDB Officer (Student Affairs Office Staff)                                                                                                                                                       |
+--------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Description:             | Enables the ÖİDB Officer to review a formal appeal submitted by a student, consult other departments (YGK/Dean's Office) if necessary, and issue a final decision (Approve/Reject) on the appeal. |
+--------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Preconditions:           | 1.  A Student has successfully submitted an appeal (Postcondition of UC-1.6: Submit Appeal).                                                                                                      |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 2.  The application's status is "Pending Appeal Review (ÖİDB)".                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 3.  The ÖİDB Officer has successfully logged in (with ÖİDB-level permissions).                                                                                                                    |
+--------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Postconditions:          | 1.  The appeal is marked as "Closed".                                                                                                                                                             |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 2.  A final decision ("Appeal Accepted" or "Appeal Rejected") and a justification note are logged.                                                                                                |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 3.  The application's final status is either updated (if appeal is accepted) or remains the same (if rejected).                                                                                   |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 4.  An automated notification is sent to the Student informing them of the appeal decision.                                                                                                       |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 5.  The student can see this new status on their dashboard (UC-1.4).                                                                                                                              |
+--------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Priority:                | Medium                                                                                                                                                                                            |
+--------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Frequency of Use:        | Low (occurs only if appeals are submitted).                                                                                                                                                       |
+--------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Normal Course of Events: | 1.  The ÖİDB Officer logs into the system.                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 2.  The dashboard shows a notification for new appeals. The Officer navigates to the "Appeals" (İtirazlar) queue.                                                                                 |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 3.  The Officer selects an appeal with the status "Pending Appeal Review (ÖİDB)" and opens it.                                                                                                    |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 4.  The system displays the student's original application, their final result (e.g., "Reddedildi"), their appeal justification text (from UC-1.6), and any supporting documents.                 |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 5.  The ÖİDB Officer reviews the student's claim against the application data and evaluation notes from YGK/Faculty Board.                                                                        |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 6.  The Officer determines the appeal is invalid (e.g., the student's calculation is wrong, or no new evidence is provided).                                                                      |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 7.  The Officer selects the "Reject Appeal" (İtirazı Reddet) option.                                                                                                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 8.  The system requires the Officer to enter a "Final Justification" (Nihai Karar Gerekçesi) text, explaining why the appeal was rejected.                                                        |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 9.  The Officer clicks "Submit Final Decision".                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 10. The system saves the decision, closes the appeal, and updates the application status to "Appeal Rejected" (Postconditions 1, 2).                                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 11. The system automatically sends a notification to the Student with the final decision and the justification (Postcondition 4).                                                                 |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 12. The use case concludes.                                                                                                                                                                       |
+--------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Alternative Courses:     | -   UC-2.6.AC.1.STEP.6 (Appeal Accepted - Simple Correction):                                                                                                                                     |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |     1.  At step 6, the ÖİDB Officer determines the appeal is valid and is a simple error ÖİDB can fix (e.g., a data entry error by ÖİDB).                                                         |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |     2.  The Officer selects the "Approve Appeal" (İtirazı Onayla) option.                                                                                                                         |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |     3.  The Officer enters a justification note.                                                                                                                                                  |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |     4.  The Officer manually corrects the student's data.                                                                                                                                         |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |     5.  The system re-calculates the student's score or status.                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |     6.  The use case proceeds from Step 9.                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | -   UC-2.6.AC.2.STEP.5 (Appeal Requires YGK/Faculty Consultation):                                                                                                                                |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |     1.  At step 5, the ÖİDB Officer determines the appeal is complex and requires academic re-evaluation (e.g., the student is appealing an intibak decision or a specific grade calculation).    |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |     2.  The Officer clicks the "Forward for Consultation" (Görüşe Gönder) button.                                                                                                                 |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |     3.  The system prompts the Officer to select the target unit (e.g., YGK UC-4.x or Dean's Office UC-5.x).                                                                                      |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |     4.  The Officer selects "YGK" and adds a note (e.g., "Lütfen öğrencinin intibak itirazını inceleyin.").                                                                                       |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |     5.  The system updates the appeal status to "Pending YGK Consultation" and routes it to the YGK queue (triggering a UC-4.x use case like "Review Appeal").                                    |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |     6.  This use case pauses. It resumes after the YGK/Faculty provides a recommendation (e.g., via UC-4.7: Add Comments).                                                                        |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |     7.  The ÖİDB Officer re-opens the appeal (now with the YGK's recommendation) and proceeds to Step 7 (Approve/Reject).                                                                         |
+--------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Exceptions:              | -   UC-2.6.EX.1 (Notification Service Fails):                                                                                                                                                     |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |     1.  At step 11, the system successfully saves the appeal decision but fails to send the automated email notification to the student.                                                          |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |     2.  The system logs the notification failure. The use case concludes successfully as the decision is logged and visible to the student via UC-1.5.                                            |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |  EX.Global.1                                                                                                                                                                                     |
+--------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Includes:                | None.                                                                                                                                                                                             |
+--------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Special Requirements:    | 1.  SR-1: All appeal decisions must be logged and require a mandatory justification note from the ÖİDB Officer.                                                                                   |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 2.  SR-2: The system must provide a mechanism to route an appeal to other departments (YGK, Dean's Office) for consultation (AC.2).                                                               |
+--------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Assumptions:             | 1.  ÖİDB is the primary coordinator for appeals, but the final academic decision on a complex appeal must come from the YGK/Faculty Board.                                                        |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          | 2.  A simple "Rejection" (Normal Flow) is the most common path.                                                                                                                                   |
+--------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Notes and Issues:        |                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
+--------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]User interface

[]

[]Performance/software quality requirements

Use Case: Review and Respond to Appeals (UC-2.6)

-   Only authorized ÖİDB Officers may view and process appeals.

-   Officers must be able to approve, reject, or forward the appeal for consultation when needed.

-   A final justification must be required and saved for all decisions (Accepted or Rejected).

-   The appeal decision and justification must be saved together without creating partial or conflicting states.

-   If consultation is used, the system must correctly route the appeal to YGK and return it to ÖİDB after feedback.

-   Appeals that are already closed must not be editable or reprocessed.

-   Sensitive fields (appeal text, justification, documents) must only be visible to authorized staff and the student.

    1.  Use case 7 for user 2

        1.  Use case template (use-case-template.docx file)

  ---------------- ----------------------------------- -------------------- --
  Use Case ID:     UC-2.7                                                   
  Use Case Name:   Monitor All Application Pipelines                        
  Created By:      Group 1                             Last Updated By:     
  Date Created:    15.11.2025                          Date Last Updated:   
  ---------------- ----------------------------------- -------------------- --

+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Actor:                   | ÖİDB Officer (Student Affairs Office Staff)                                                                                                                                                                                  |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Description:             | Enables the ÖİDB Officer to access a high-level administrative dashboard to monitor the real-time status, distribution, and bottlenecks of all applications in the system's workflow.                                        |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Preconditions:           | 1.  The ÖİDB Officer has successfully logged in (with ÖİDB-level permissions).                                                                                                                                               |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 2.  There are applications in the system in various states (e.g., "Pending ÖİDB", "Pending YGK", "Closed").                                                                                                                  |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Postconditions:          | 1.  The ÖİDB Officer is informed of the overall health and status of the application period.                                                                                                                                 |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 2.  The ÖİDB Officer has identified potential bottlenecks (e.g., delays in a specific department or process step).                                                                                                           |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 3.  The ÖİDB Officer may have taken administrative action (like sending reminders) to resolve delays.                                                                                                                        |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Priority:                | High (for process management)                                                                                                                                                                                                |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Frequency of Use:        | Daily (or multiple times per day) by ÖİDB managers during the application period.                                                                                                                                            |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Normal Course of Events: | 1.  The ÖİDB Officer logs into the system.                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 2.  The Officer navigates to the "Administrative Dashboard" or "Pipeline Monitor" (İş Akışı Takibi) screen.                                                                                                                  |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 3.  The system displays the main dashboard, which includes several real-time (or near real-time) widgets.                                                                                                                    |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 4.  Widget 1 (Status Distribution): The Officer views a pie chart or bar graph showing the distribution of all applications by their current status (e.g., 30% Pending ÖİDB, 20% Pending YDYO, 40% Pending YGK, 10% Closed). |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 5.  Widget 2 (Bottleneck/Alerts): The Officer views a list of "delayed" applications (e.g., "35 Applications waiting in 'Pending YGK' queue for > 5 days").                                                                  |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 6.  The Officer clicks on a segment of the status chart (e.g., "Pending YGK") or an alert (e.g., the "35 Applications" link).                                                                                                |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 7.  The system displays a filtered list of all applications in that specific state (e.g., the 35 applications waiting for YGK).                                                                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 8.  The Officer analyzes the list to identify the source of the delay (e.g., all 35 applications are for the "Mimarlık" department).                                                                                         |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 9.  The Officer selects the relevant applications (or all 35) and clicks the "Send Reminder" (Hatırlatma Gönder) button.                                                                                                     |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 10. The system sends an automated notification to the responsible unit (e.g., the Dean's Office (UC-5.x) or YGK (UC-4.x) for the "Mimarlık" department).                                                                     |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 11. The use case concludes, as the Officer has successfully monitored the pipeline and taken corrective action.                                                                                                              |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Alternative Courses:     | -   UC-2.7.AC.1.STEP.8 (Officer Manually Contacts Unit):                                                                                                                                                                     |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          |     1.  At step 8, after identifying the bottleneck, the Officer chooses not to use the system's "Send Reminder" button.                                                                                                     |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          |     2.  The Officer takes action outside the system (e.g., calls the Dean's Office directly).                                                                                                                                |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          |     3.  The use case concludes at step 8.                                                                                                                                                                                    |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | -   UC-2.7.AC.2.STEP.3 (Officer Filters Dashboard):                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          |     1.  At step 3, the Officer wishes to see the pipeline for only one faculty.                                                                                                                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          |     2.  The Officer uses the dashboard's filter controls (e.g., "Filter by Faculty: Mühendislik Fakültesi").                                                                                                                 |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          |     3.  The system reloads the widgets (Step 4, 5) showing data only for that faculty.                                                                                                                                       |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          |     4.  The use case continues from Step 6.                                                                                                                                                                                  |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Exceptions:              |  EX.Global.5                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                          | UC-2.7.EX.2 (Reminder Service Fails):                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 1.  At step 10, the Officer clicks "Send Reminder", but the notification service fails.                                                                                                                                      |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 2.  The system displays an error: "Hatırlatma gönderilemedi. (Hata Kodu: 272-NOTIFY)"                                                                                                                                        |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 3.  The use case returns to step 9.                                                                                                                                                                                          |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Includes:                | None.                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Special Requirements:    | 1.  SR-1: The dashboard data must be real-time or updated very frequently (e.g., cached no longer than 5 minutes).                                                                                                           |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 2.  SR-2: The dashboard must provide clear, visual graphs for easy interpretation.                                                                                                                                           |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 3.  SR-3: The dashboard must allow "drill-down" functionality (clicking a graph segment to see a detailed list).                                                                                                             |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 4.  SR-4: The system must define "delay" thresholds (e.g., 5 days in YGK) (configurable via UC-7.4).                                                                                                                         |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Assumptions:             | 1.  This dashboard is only visible to high-level ÖİDB staff (Managers) and not standard Officers. (This needs confirmation).                                                                                                 |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          | 2.  All actions (like UC-2.1, UC-3.1, UC-4.1) correctly update the application status, which this dashboard reads from.                                                                                                      |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Notes and Issues:        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]User interface

[]

Performance/software quality requirements

Use Case: Monitor All Application Pipelines (UC-2.7)

-   Access to language decision data for applications in this use case must be restricted to authenticated users with ÖİDB permissions (and other authorized staff, if defined by policy).

-   The language decision and justification coming from YDYO must be stored as a consistent, tamper-evident record and must exactly match the original decision recorded by YDYO.

-   Any change of status or notes related to language evaluation (e.g., “Language Approved”, “Language Failed”, “Exempt”) must be logged with Officer ID (or system worker ID), Application ID, timestamp, and the old/new values.

-   Language documents and decision notes handled in this use case must be treated as personal data and protected according to KVKK and institutional data retention policies.

    1.  Use cases for user 3

        1.  Use case 1 for user 3

            1.  Use case template (use-case-template.docx file)

  ---------------- -------------------------------------- -------------------- --
  Use Case ID:     UC-3.1                                                      
  Use Case Name:   Review Language Proficiency Document                        
  Created By:      Group 1                                Last Updated By:     
  Date Created:    16.11.2025                             Date Last Updated:   
  ---------------- -------------------------------------- -------------------- --

+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Actor:                   | YDYO Officer (Foreign Languages Department Staff)                                                                                                                                                        |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Description:             | Enables the YDYO Officer to review the student's uploaded English proficiency document (e.g., TOEFL, PTE, YDS) and determine if it meets the university's language requirements.                         |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Preconditions:           | 1.  The application has been forwarded by ÖİDB to YDYO (Postcondition of UC-2.3: Forward Application to YDYO).                                                                                           |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 2.  The application's status is "In Review (YDYO)".                                                                                                                                                      |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 3.  The YDYO Officer has successfully logged in (with YDYO-level permissions).                                                                                                                           |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Postconditions:          | 1.  A final language decision ("Successful", "Unsuccessful", or "Exempt") is saved to the application record.                                                                                            |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 2.  The application's language status is updated (e.g., "Language Approved", "Language Failed").                                                                                                         |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 3.  An automated notification/status update is sent to the ÖİDB dashboard (UC-2.7) and becomes visible in the student's timeline (UC-1.4).                                                               |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 4.  The application is removed from the YDYO "Pending Review" queue.                                                                                                                                     |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Priority:                | High                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Frequency of Use:        | Once for every application routed to YDYO.                                                                                                                                                               |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Normal Course of Events: | 1.  The YDYO Officer logs into the system.                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 2.  The YDYO dashboard displays a queue of applications with the status "In Review (YDYO)".                                                                                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 3.  The Officer selects an application from the queue and opens it.                                                                                                                                      |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 4.  The system displays the "Language Verification" screen, showing the student's relevant data and the uploaded language document(s) (from UC-1.2).                                                     |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 5.  The YDYO Officer reviews the document (e.g., a TOEFL score report).                                                                                                                                  |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 6.  The Officer manually checks the document's validity against institutional rules:                                                                                                                     |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |     -   Authenticity (e.g., via test ID lookup, if available).                                                                                                                                           |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |     -   Validity Period (e.g., "Sınav 2 yıldan eski mi?").                                                                                                                                               |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |     -   Score (e.g., "TOEFL > 79 mı?").                                                                                                                                                                  |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 7.  The Officer determines the student meets the requirement.                                                                                                                                            |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 8.  The Officer selects the "Successful" (Başarılı) option.                                                                                                                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 9.  The Officer clicks the "Submit Decision" (Kararı Kaydet) button.                                                                                                                                     |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 10. The system saves the "Successful" decision, updates the application's language status to "Language Approved", and notifies ÖİDB (Postconditions).                                                    |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | 11. The use case concludes.                                                                                                                                                                              |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Alternative Courses:     | -   UC-3.1.AC.1.STEP.6 (Decision: Unsuccessful/Invalid):                                                                                                                                                 |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |     1.  At step 6, the YDYO Officer determines the document is invalid (e.g., score is too low, exam is expired, document is fraudulent) or the document is missing entirely.                            |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |     2.  The Officer selects the "Unsuccessful" (Başarısız / Geçersiz Belge) option.                                                                                                                      |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |     3.  The system requires the Officer to add a mandatory note (see AC.3) explaining the reason for failure (e.g., "TOEFL skoru 65, minimum 79 gerekli.").                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |     4.  The Officer clicks "Submit Decision".                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |     5.  The system saves the "Unsuccessful" decision, updates the language status to "Language Failed", and notifies ÖİDB (Postconditions).                                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |     6.  The use case concludes.                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          | -   UC-3.1.AC.2.STEP.6 (Decision: Exempt):                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |     1.  At step 6, the YDYO Officer determines the student is exempt from the language requirement (e.g., they are transferring from a 100% English-instruction university).                             |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |     2.  The Officer selects the "Exempt" (Muaf) option.                                                                                                                                                  |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |     3.  The system requires the Officer to add a mandatory note (see AC.3) explaining the reason for exemption (e.g., "Muaf - %100 İngilizce Program").                                                  |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |     4.  The Officer clicks "Submit Decision".                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
