+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Includes:                | UC-6.4: Add Board-Level Notes (Optionally included in AC.2).                                                                                                                                                                 |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Special Requirements:    | SR-1: The "Approve" action must be logged with the specific member's name and a timestamp (digital signature).                                                                                                               |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Assumptions:             | A single "Approve" action (this use case) is final and represents the entire board's decision.                                                                                                                               |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Notes and Issues:        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

1.  []User interface

2.  Performance/software quality requirements

Use Case: Approve Package (UC-6.2)

-   The approval workflow must be atomic: the package status cannot change unless the digital signature is successfully stored.

-   Only authenticated Faculty Board Members may access and execute the approval action.

-   The system must enforce non-repudiation by logging member identity, timestamp, and signature data.

-   The confirmation interface must clearly show the member’s identity and the action being finalized.

-   Signature failures must follow the standardized 621-SIGN error code format and allow safe rollback.

-   Notification failures must not block approval; the decision is prioritized over messaging.

-   All approval events must be logged with Member ID, Package ID, Timestamp, Signature Hash, and IP Address.

-   Once approved, the package must be locked against further edits and move only forward in workflow.

    1.  Use case 3 for user 6

        1.  Use case template (use-case-template.docx file)

  ---------------- ---------------- -------------------- --
  Use Case ID:     UC-6.3                                
  Use Case Name:   Reject Package                        
  Created By:      Group 1          Last Updated By:     
  Date Created:    17.11.2025       Date Last Updated:   
  ---------------- ---------------- -------------------- --

+--------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Actor:                   | Faculty Board Member                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
+--------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Description:             | Enables the Faculty Board (represented by an authorized member) to formally reject an evaluation package due to errors or policy issues, provide a mandatory reason, and route it back to the YGK for correction.             |
+--------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Preconditions:           | 1.  The Board Member has reviewed the package (Postcondition of UC-6.1: Review Evaluation Package).                                                                                                                           |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 2.  The package's status is "Pending Faculty Board Review".                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 3.  The Board Member is logged in.                                                                                                                                                                                            |
+--------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Postconditions:          | 1.  The Faculty Board's "Reject" decision is logged and digitally signed.                                                                                                                                                     |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 2.  A mandatory rejection note (from UC-6.4) is attached to the package.                                                                                                                                                      |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 3.  The package status is updated to "Rejected by Faculty Board" (veya "Waiting for YGK Correction").                                                                                                                         |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 4.  The package is routed back to the YGK work queue (triggering UC-4.9: Review Clarification Request).                                                                                                                       |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 5.  The Dean's Office is notified of the rejection/return.                                                                                                                                                                    |
+--------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Priority:                | Medium                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
+--------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Frequency of Use:        | Low (occurs only if YGK packages contain critical errors).                                                                                                                                                                    |
+--------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Normal Course of Events: | 1.  The Faculty Board Member has completed their review (UC-6.1).                                                                                                                                                             |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 2.  The Member clicks the "Reject" (Reddet) button (Taslak Step 1).                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 3.  The system initiates UC-6.4: Add Board-Level Notes.                                                                                                                                                                       |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 4.  The system displays a mandatory text field for the rejection reason (as per the taslak, Main Flow, Step 2).                                                                                                               |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 5.  The Member types the rejection reason (e.g., "İntibak tablosu YÖK mevzuatına aykırı, YGK tarafından yeniden düzenlenmeli.").                                                                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 6.  The Member saves the note (completing the UC-6.4 steps).                                                                                                                                                                  |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 7.  The system updates the package status to "Rejected by Faculty Board" (Postcondition 3 / Taslak Step 4).                                                                                                                   |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 8.  The system routes the package (with the new note) back to the YGK work queue (triggering UC-4.9) (Postcondition 4 / Taslak Step 3).                                                                                       |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 9.  The system sends automated notifications to the Dean's Office (Postcondition 5).                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 10. The use case concludes.                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
+--------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Alternative Courses:     |  UC-6.3.AC.1.STEP.2 (Member Cancels Rejection):                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 1.  At step 2, the Member clicks "Reject" by mistake.                                                                                                                                                                         |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 2.  At step 4 (the UC-6.4 modal), the Member clicks "Cancel" (İptal).                                                                                                                                                         |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 3.  The use case terminates and returns control to the parent use case (e.g., UC-6.1), allowing the member to "Approve" (UC-6.2) instead.                                                                                     |
+--------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Exceptions:              |  UC-6.3.EX.1 (YGK Queue Fails):                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 1.  At step 8, the system saves the rejection status, but fails to route the package back to the YGK queue.                                                                                                                   |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 2.  The system displays an error: "Paket YGK kuyruğuna geri gönderilemedi. (Hata Kodu: 631-QUEUE)".                                                                                                                           |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 3.  The use case terminates (requires Admin intervention).                                                                                                                                                                    |
+--------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Includes:                | UC-6.4: Add Board-Level Notes (This is mandatorily included to create the rejection reason, as per the taslak, Main Flow, Step 2).                                                                                            |
+--------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Special Requirements:    | 1.  SR-1: The rejection reason (the note from UC-6.4) must be mandatory for this action.                                                                                                                                      |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 2.  SR-2: This action must trigger the loop back to the YGK (UC-4.9) for correction (as per the taslak, "Postconditions").                                                                                                    |
+--------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Assumptions:             | A "Reject" by the Faculty Board is a "soft reject" (i.e., "send back for correction"), not a "hard reject" (i.e., "fail the application"). This matches the taslak's Postcondition: "YGK'dan yeniden düzenleme talep edilir." |
+--------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Notes and Issues:        | 1.  This use case is mutually exclusive with UC-6.2 (Approve).                                                                                                                                                                |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                          | 2.  This use case triggers the UC-4.9 (YGK Review Clarification) loop, sending the package backward in the workflow.                                                                                                          |
+--------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

1.  User interface

2.  Performance/software quality requirements

Use Case: Reject Package (UC-6.3)

-   The rejection workflow must be atomic: the status cannot change to Rejected by Faculty Board unless routing back to the YGK queue succeeds.

-   Only authenticated Faculty Board Members may access and execute the rejection action.

-   The system must enforce a mandatory justification note before finalizing rejection.

-   Error handling must follow the standardized 631-QUEUE schema and allow safe rollback.

-   The system must reopen the package for YGK corrections while keeping it locked for the Dean’s Office.

-   All rejection events must be fully logged: Member ID, Package ID, Timestamp, and Rejection Reason.

-   Routing failures must trigger a critical alert, as they prevent the correction workflow from proceeding.

    1.  Use case 4 for user 6

        1.  Use case template (use-case-template.docx file)

  ---------------- ----------------------- -------------------- --
  Use Case ID:     UC-6.4                                       
  Use Case Name:   Add Board-Level Notes                        
  Created By:      Group 1                 Last Updated By:     
  Date Created:    17.11.2025              Date Last Updated:   
  ---------------- ----------------------- -------------------- --

+--------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Actor:                   | Faculty Board Member                                                                                                                                                                                      |
+--------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Description:             | Enables a Faculty Board Member to add an official, visible note (justification or comment) to an evaluation package. This note becomes part of the permanent record visible to the Dean's Office and YGK. |
+--------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Preconditions:           | 1.  The Board Member is currently reviewing a package in a parent use case (e.g., UC-6.1, UC-6.2, or UC-6.3).                                                                                             |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          | 2.  The "Add Note" action has been triggered (either optionally by the user or mandatorily by the system, e.g., for UC-6.3: Reject Package).                                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          | 3.  The Board Member is logged in.                                                                                                                                                                        |
+--------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Postconditions:          | 1.  The note (text) is saved and logged with the Board Member's name and a timestamp.                                                                                                                     |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          | 2.  The note is associated with the Application Package.                                                                                                                                                  |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          | 3.  The system makes the note visible to the Dean's Office and YGK (as per the taslak).                                                                                                                   |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          | 4.  The system returns control to the parent use case (e.g., UC-6.3, step 7).                                                                                                                             |
+--------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Priority:                | Medium (High when mandatory for UC-6.3)                                                                                                                                                                   |
+--------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Frequency of Use:        | Used whenever a package is rejected, or optionally when reviewed/approved.                                                                                                                                |
+--------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Normal Course of Events: | 1.  This use case is "included" by the parent use case UC-6.3: Reject Package.                                                                                                                            |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          | 2.  The system displays the "Add Board-Level Note" (Kurul Notu Ekle) interface (e.g., a modal text box).                                                                                                  |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          | 3.  The system displays a prompt: "Red kararı için gerekçe girilmesi zorunludur." (A justification is mandatory for this decision).                                                                       |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          | 4.  The Board Member types the justification (kararla ilgili ek yorum) into the text field (e.g., "İntibak tablosu YÖK mevzuatına aykırı, YGK tarafından yeniden düzenlenmeli.").                         |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          | 5.  The Board Member clicks the "Save Note" (Notu Kaydet) button.                                                                                                                                         |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          | 6.  The system validates that the text field is not empty.                                                                                                                                                |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          | 7.  The system saves the note to the database, linking it to the package, user, and timestamp (Postconditions 1, 2, 3).                                                                                   |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          | 8.  The system closes the "Add Note" interface and returns control to the parent use case (UC-6.3) (Postcondition 4). The use case concludes.                                                             |
+--------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Alternative Courses:     | -   UC-6.4.AC.1 (Optional Note - e.g., During Review):                                                                                                                                                    |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          |     1.  This use case is "included" by a parent use case (e.g., UC-6.1.AC.1) because the Member optionally clicked the "Add Note" button.                                                                 |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          |     2.  The system displays the "Add Board-Level Note" interface (Step 2).                                                                                                                                |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          |     3.  The prompt (Step 3) is "İsteğe bağlı not (Dekanlık ve YGK görebilir):" (Optional Note...).                                                                                                        |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          |     4.  The use case proceeds from Step 4 (the validation in Step 6 is skipped).                                                                                                                          |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          | -   UC-6.4.AC.2.STEP.6 (Empty Mandatory Note):                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          |     1.  At step 6 (Normal Flow), the system validates the note (for a mandatory submission) and finds the text field is empty.                                                                            |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          |     2.  The system displays an inline error: "Lütfen kararınız için bir gerekçe girin."                                                                                                                   |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          |     3.  The use case returns to Step 4.                                                                                                                                                                   |
+--------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Exceptions:              |  EX.Global.1                                                                                                                                                                                             |
|                          |  EX.Global.5                                                                                                                                                                                             |
+--------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Includes:                | None. (This is a utility use case that is included by others).                                                                                                                                            |
+--------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Special Requirements:    | 1.  SR-1: The visibility of the note must be restricted (as per the taslak: Dekanlık + YGK). The student should not see these notes.                                                                      |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                          | 2.  SR-2: Notes must be logged (timestamped) and immutable (cannot be edited after saving).                                                                                                               |
+--------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Assumptions:             | This use case cannot be initiated by itself; it is always called from within another Faculty Board use case.                                                                                              |
+--------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Notes and Issues:        |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
+--------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

1.  []User interface

2.  Performance/software quality requirements

Use Case: Add Board-Level Notes (UC-6.4)

-   Saving a note must be atomic: the parent action (e.g., Reject) cannot proceed unless the note is successfully stored.

-   Only authorized Faculty Board Members may create board-level notes; notes must remain hidden from Students and ÖİDB.

-   Saved notes must be immutable and permanently read-only to preserve the legal audit trail.

-   The system must enforce mandatory note entry when required (e.g., during rejection) before allowing save.

-   Error handling must follow the standardized 641-DB schema and prevent incomplete workflow transitions.

-   Concurrent note creation during board meetings must not lock or block the package record.

-   All note creation events must be stored in the audit trail and available for administrative review.

    1.  Use case 5 for user 6

        1.  Use case template (use-case-template.docx file)

  ---------------- ------------------------------------ -------------------- --
  Use Case ID:     UC-6.5                                                    
  Use Case Name:   Confirm Final List for Publication                        
  Created By:      Group 1                              Last Updated By:     
  Date Created:    17.11.2025                           Date Last Updated:   
  ---------------- ------------------------------------ -------------------- --

+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Actor:                   | Faculty Board Member                                                                                                                                                                                       |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Description:             | Enables the Faculty Board (represented by an authorized member) to give the final confirmation that the approved lists (Asil/Yedek) are ready to be published to students by ÖİDB.                         |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Preconditions:           | 1.  The evaluation package has been officially approved by the Faculty Board (Postcondition of UC-6.2: Approve Package).                                                                                   |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          | 2.  The package's status is "Approved (Faculty Board)".                                                                                                                                                    |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          | 3.  The Board Member is logged in.                                                                                                                                                                         |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Postconditions:          | 1.  The package status is updated to "Ready for Publication" (Yayınlanmaya Hazır).                                                                                                                         |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          | 2.  An automated notification is sent to the ÖİDB dashboard.                                                                                                                                               |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          | 3.  This action (and its postconditions) triggers the start of UC-2.5: Publish Results.                                                                                                                    |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Priority:                | High                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Frequency of Use:        | Once per package, immediately after UC-6.2.                                                                                                                                                                |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Normal Course of Events: | 1.  Immediately after completing UC-6.2 (Approve Package), the system displays the "Approved (Faculty Board)" package screen.                                                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          | 2.  The system displays the final lists (Asil/Yedek/Red) (Taslak Step 1).                                                                                                                                  |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          | 3.  The system also displays the "Confirm for Publication" (Yayınlama Onayı Ver) button.                                                                                                                   |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          | 4.  The Board Member (e.g., Dean) gives the lists a final visual check.                                                                                                                                    |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          | 5.  The Member clicks the "Confirm for Publication" button (Taslak Step 2).                                                                                                                                |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          | 6.  The system displays a final confirmation prompt: "Bu onay, listelerin ÖİDB tarafından öğrencilere duyurulması için son adımdır. Onaylıyor musunuz?"                                                    |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          | 7.  The Member confirms the action.                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          | 8.  The system updates the package status to "Ready for Publication" (Postcondition 1).                                                                                                                    |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          | 9.  The system sends an automated notification to the ÖİDB dashboard: "Mühendislik Fakültesi paketleri yayınlanmaya hazır." (Taslak Step 3 / Postcondition 2).                                             |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          | 10. The use case concludes, triggering UC-2.5.                                                                                                                                                             |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Alternative Courses:     |  UC-6.5.AC.1.STEP.4 (Member Decides to Add a Final Note):                                                                                                                                                 |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          | 1.  At step 4, before confirming publication, the Member wants to add a final note for ÖİDB.                                                                                                               |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          | 2.  The Member first initiates UC-6.4: Add Board-Level Notes (e.g., "Yedek 3. sıradaki öğrencinin durumu X'e bağlıdır.").                                                                                  |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          | 3.  After saving the note, the use case returns to Step 4.                                                                                                                                                 |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Exceptions:              | -   UC-6.5.EX.1 (ÖİDB Notification Fails):                                                                                                                                                                 |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          |     1.  At step 9, the system successfully updates the package status but fails to send the automated notification to the ÖİDB dashboard.                                                                  |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          |     2.  The system logs the notification error. The use case concludes successfully, as ÖİDB'nin dashboard'u ("Ready for Publication" kuyruğu) yine de güncellenmiş olacaktır (Postcondition 1 sayesinde). |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          |  EX.Global.1                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          |  EX.Global.5                                                                                                                                                                                              |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Includes:                | UC-6.4: Add Board-Level Notes (Optionally included in AC.1).                                                                                                                                               |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Special Requirements:    | SR-1: This "Confirm" button (Step 5) must only be available to a user with high authority (e.g., Dean or designated Board Chair), as defined in UC-7.1.                                                    |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Assumptions:             | 1.  This use case is a final "sign-off" step that formally transfers responsibility from the Faculty Board to ÖİDB for the act of publication.                                                             |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                          | 2.  UC-6.2 (Approve Package) was the decision, and this (UC-6.5) is the confirmation of that decision for publication.                                                                                     |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Notes and Issues:        |                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
+--------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

1.  []User interface

2.  Performance/software quality requirements

Use Case: Confirm Final List for Publication (UC-6.5)

-   The status update to “Ready for Publication” must be atomic; if the save fails, the package must remain in “Approved (Faculty Board)”.

-   Only authorized high-level roles (Dean / Board Chair) may access the Confirm for Publication action.

-   Error handling must follow the standardized 651-DB code and block incomplete state transitions.

-   The status change must immediately make the package visible to the ÖİDB publication workflow.

-   Any existing board-level notes must remain securely attached and visible to ÖİDB after confirmation.

-   All confirmation actions must be logged with member identity, timestamp, and action type.

-   Notification failures must not rollback the approval; they should only generate warning logs.

    1.  Use case 6 for user 6

        1.  Use case template (use-case-template.docx file)

  ---------------- ----------------------------------------- -------------------- --
  Use Case ID:     UC-6.6                                                         
  Use Case Name:   Track All Packages Pending or Completed                        
  Created By:      Group 1                                   Last Updated By:     
  Date Created:    17.11.2025                                Date Last Updated:   
  ---------------- ----------------------------------------- -------------------- --

+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Actor:                   | Faculty Board Member                                                                                                                                                                   |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Description:             | Enables a Faculty Board Member to access a high-level dashboard to monitor the status of all evaluation packages (pending, approved, or rejected) within the Faculty Board's workflow. |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Preconditions:           | 1.  The Board Member has successfully logged in (with Faculty Board permissions).                                                                                                      |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          | 2.  At least one package has been forwarded to the Faculty Board (Postcondition of UC-5.3).                                                                                            |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Postconditions:          | The Board Member is informed of the overall status of all packages in their workflow (as per the taslak: "Kurul tüm süreçleri şeffaf şekilde takip eder.").                            |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Priority:                | Medium                                                                                                                                                                                 |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Frequency of Use:        | Daily (or multiple times per day) by Board members during the evaluation period.                                                                                                       |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Normal Course of Events: | 1.  The Faculty Board Member logs into the system.                                                                                                                                     |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          | 2.  The Member navigates to the "Board Dashboard" (Kurul Dashboard) screen (Taslak Step 1).                                                                                            |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          | 3.  The system displays a list or set of widgets for all packages associated with their Faculty.                                                                                       |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          | 4.  The system categorizes the packages by their current status (Taslak Step 2):                                                                                                       |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          |     -   "Pending Faculty Board Review" (Bekleyen Paketler - from UC-5.3)                                                                                                               |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          |     -   "Approved (by Board)" (Onaylanan Paketler - from UC-6.2)                                                                                                                       |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          |     -   "Rejected (by Board)" (YGK'ya İade Edilen Paketler - from UC-6.3)                                                                                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          | 5.  The Board Member clicks on a status (e.g., "Pending...") or a specific package to view details.                                                                                    |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          | 6.  The system displays the list of packages in that state, or opens the specific package (triggering UC-6.1).                                                                         |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          | 7.  The Board Member can also review "Approved" or "Rejected" packages to see past decisions (Taslak Step 3: "Geçmiş paketler incelenebilir.").                                        |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          | 8.  The use case concludes, as the Member is informed (Postcondition 1).                                                                                                               |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Alternative Courses:     |  UC-6.6.AC.1.STEP.5 (Member Drills Down to Review a Pending Package):                                                                                                                 |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          | 1.  At step 5, the Member clicks on a package in the "Pending Faculty Board Review" list.                                                                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          | 2.  This use case terminates.                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          | 3.  The system initiates UC-6.1: Review Evaluation Package for that specific package.                                                                                                  |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Exceptions:              |  EX.Global.1                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                          |  EX.Global.5                                                                                                                                                                          |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Includes:                | None. (This is a read-only reporting/monitoring use case that can trigger UC-6.1).                                                                                                     |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Special Requirements:    | 1.  SR-1: The dashboard must be real-time.                                                                                                                                             |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          | 2.  SR-2: The dashboard must allow "drill-down" functionality (clicking a list to see details or a package).                                                                           |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Assumptions:             | 1.  This dashboard is the main landing page for the Faculty Board Member after logging in.                                                                                             |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                          | 2.  This dashboard provides a high-level package view, whereas UC-5.6 (Dean's Office track) provides a more granular application view.                                                 |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Notes and Issues:        | This is the final use case for the Faculty Board actor.                                                                                                                                |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

1.  []User interface

2.  Performance/software quality requirements

Use Case: Track All Packages Pending or Completed (UC-6.6)

-   Dashboard must always show real-time, accurate counts matching the database; no stale or cached data is acceptable.

-   Only authenticated Faculty Board Members can access this dashboard, and it must show only their Faculty’s packages.

-   Historical (completed) packages must remain accessible without performance degradation.

-   Dashboard queries must scale efficiently as archived packages accumulate over years.

-   Error handling must follow standardized 661-DB rules and fail gracefully without freezing the interface.

-   Dashboard access must be logged with Member ID and timestamp to ensure oversight traceability.

-   Data displayed must stay consistent with the Dean’s Office dashboard—every status must match across both views.

    1.  Use cases for user 7

        1.  Use case 1 for user 7

            1.  Use case template (use-case-template.docx file)

  ---------------- --------------------------------- -------------------- --
  Use Case ID:     UC-7.1                                                 
  Use Case Name:   Manage User Roles & Permissions                        
  Created By:      Group 1                           Last Updated By:     
  Date Created:    18.11.2025                        Date Last Updated:   
  ---------------- --------------------------------- -------------------- --

+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Actor:                   | System Admin                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Description:             | Enables the System Admin to create, view, search, and assign user roles (e.g., Student, ÖİDB, YGK) and manage the specific permissions associated with each role.                                              |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Preconditions:           | The System Admin has successfully logged into the system (with Admin-level privileges).                                                                                                                        |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Postconditions:          | 1.  The user's role and/or permissions are updated in the system.                                                                                                                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                          | 2.  The user's new permissions take effect at their next login (or immediately, if the session is refreshed).                                                                                                  |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Priority:                | High (Critical for system security and operation)                                                                                                                                                              |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Frequency of Use:        | Low (Used when new staff are added/removed or when permissions change).                                                                                                                                        |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Normal Course of Events: | 1.  The System Admin logs into the "Admin Panel".                                                                                                                                                              |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                          | 2.  The Admin navigates to the "User Management" (Kullanıcı Yönetimi) screen (Taslak Step 1).                                                                                                                  |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                          | 3.  The Admin searches for a specific user (e.g., by TCKN or Name) or views the full user list (Taslak Step 2).                                                                                                |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                          | 4.  The Admin clicks "Edit" (Düzenle) next to a user's name (e.g., "Ahmet Mete Yazıcı").                                                                                                                       |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                          | 5.  The system displays the user's profile and their current roles.                                                                                                                                            |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                          | 6.  The Admin selects (or de-selects) a role from the role assignment checklist (Taslak Step 3):                                                                                                               |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                          |     -   Student                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
